text: Lesson 20
Areas Where AI Can Support Public Health Activities
textPreview: Areas Where AI Can Support Public Health Activities

text: Staff Training Module
AI Playbook and Toolkit for Public Health Departments
textPreview: AI Playbook and Toolkit for Public Health Departments

alt: AI Playbook for Public Health logo

text:
Welcome participants to the Areas Where AI Can Support Public Health Activities lesson.
Explain that this deck is designed for staff training, leadership orientation, governance preparation, or cross-program discussion.
Frame the topic in plain language: Expanded examples showing how AI can support specific public health activities, what safeguards are needed, and which plays and tools help agencies move from idea to responsible implementation.
Invite participants to think about where this topic already appears in their department's workflows, vendor tools, data systems, communications, or planning conversations.
AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT
Why this topic matters
Expanded examples showing how AI can support specific public health activities, what safeguards are needed, and which plays and tools help agencies move from idea to responsible implementation.
textPreview: Expanded examples showing how AI can support specific public health activities, what safeguards are needed, and which plays and tools hel...

text: Public health value

Support areas help staff move from abstract AI concepts to concrete public health opportunities. They should be used to identify possible use cases, not to skip readiness, governance, stakeholder engagement, or risk review.
textPreview: Support areas help staff move from abstract AI concepts to concrete public health opportunities. They should be used to identify possible...

text: Guardrail
These examples are planning prompts, not automatic recommendations to adopt AI without readiness assessment, governance review, stakeholder engagement, and monitoring.
textPreview: These examples are planning prompts, not automatic recommendations to adopt AI without readiness assessment, governance review, stakehold...

text:
Use this slide in the Areas Where AI Can Support Public Health Activities training module.
Use this slide to explain the practical public health reason for learning this topic before AI tools are approved or deployed.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 2, 3, 4, 7, 11, 12, 13 and Tools 3, 7, 13, 14, 19, 20, 22, 37, 39, 42.
Emphasize the core guardrail: These examples are planning prompts, not automatic recommendations to adopt AI without readiness assessment, governance review, stakeholder engagement, and monitoring.
Ask participants how this topic could affect their own program area, staff roles, community relationships, data, or public communications.
AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT
Learning objectives
By the end of this lesson, staff should be able to:
textPreview: By the end of this lesson, staff should be able to:

text:
• Explain Areas Where AI Can Support Public Health Activities in plain language.
• Identify appropriate public health uses and limits.
• Describe human review, governance, equity, privacy, and monitoring expectations.
• Connect the topic to the related plays and tools.

text:
Use this slide in the Areas Where AI Can Support Public Health Activities training module.
Use this slide to make the topic concrete through public health workflow examples.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 2, 3, 4, 7, 11, 12, 13 and Tools 3, 7, 13, 14, 19, 20, 22, 37, 39, 42.
For each example, ask: What problem is being solved? What data would be used? Who reviews the AI output? What decision remains human? What could go wrong?
AI PLAYBOOK AND TOOLKIT
Facilitated discussion and exercise
Discussion questions

Which support areas align with current public health priorities? Which areas are feasible given current data, workforce, governance, and funding? Which areas could create equity, privacy, transparency, or public trust concerns?

text: Training exercise

text:
Use this slide in the Areas Where AI Can Support Public Health Activities training module.
Use this slide for a facilitated activity near the end of the lesson.
Connect the discussion back to the playbook: this topic supports Plays 2, 3, 4, 7, 11, 12, 13 and Tools 3, 7, 13, 14, 19, 20, 22, 37, 39, 42.
Give participants time to work individually or in small groups. Ask them to produce a practical artifact that can feed into readiness assessment, governance review, use case prioritization, or implementation planning.
Expected takeaways include: AI opportunity list, Use case screening notes, Prioritized concepts for governance review.
